syllabusPoints.sourceReference: p.12, Core subject content 1 Number, C1.1 Types of number
reviewNotes: Official point requires identifying and using natural numbers, integers, prime numbers, square numbers, cube numbers, common factors, common multiples, rational/irrational numbers and reciprocals.
Mapped square/cube numbers to the powers-and-roots nodes for squares/square roots and cubes/cube roots; factors/multiples node covers common factors and common multiples.
C1.1 is Core content and is also examinable in the Extended tier because Extended includes all Core content.
The syllabus does not fix individual topics to specific papers, only to tiers (Core: Papers 1 and 3; Extended: Papers 2 and 4).
Ignored illustrative examples in the Notes and examples column (number/word conversion, prime factorisation, HCF, LCM) as non-syllabus content.
Venn diagrams limited to two sets; mapped to set-language, Venn-diagram, union/intersection and complement nodes.
Includes recall of squares/cubes and roots; other powers/roots covered by positive-integer indices node.
Covers language/notation, equivalence and conversion between fractions, decimals and percentages; recurring-decimal conversion is excluded.
Ordering node covers decimal ordering; inequality symbols also link to inequality notation not captured here.
Includes four operations, directed numbers, order of operations and brackets across integers, fractions and decimals.
Integer indices only (positive, zero, negative) and rules of indices; fractional indices are Extended.
Note states Core standard-form calculations appear on Paper 3, but conversion/interpretation are not paper-fixed.
Combines rounding to decimal places/significant figures/powers of ten and estimation.
Only upper/lower bounds for rounded data; bounds of calculated results are excluded.
Covers simplest form, sharing in a ratio and proportional reasoning in context.
Includes common and other rate measures; required formulas are given in questions.
Covers percentage of quantity, one quantity as percentage of another, increase/decrease and simple/compound interest.
Calculator efficiency, entering values and interpreting displays are not represented by a specific canonical node.
Includes time-unit calculations, 12/24-hour clocks and timetables/time zones.
Money calculations are covered; currency conversion has no dedicated node.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Letters as generalised numbers, substitution and formulas.
Collecting like terms, expanding single and paired brackets, and full factorisation.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Algebraic integer indices (positive, zero, negative); logarithms are not required.
Constructing linear expressions/equations, solving linear equations, two linear simultaneous equations and changing the subject.
Represent and interpret inequalities on a number line; solving linear inequalities is implied.
Term-to-term rules and nth terms for linear, simple quadratic and simple cubic sequences.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Practical graphs (travel/conversion) and drawing from data; linear-graph node covers interpretation, modelling node covers context.
Linear, quadratic and reciprocal graphs; solving associated equations graphically.
Recognise, sketch and interpret linear and quadratic graphs; turning points not required.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Use and interpret Cartesian coordinates in two dimensions.
Drawing straight-line graphs from linear equations, chiefly y = mx + c.
Gradient from a grid only.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Obtain/interpret equation y = mx + c and vertical lines x = k; give simplified form.
Parallel line gradient/equation linked to equal gradients; no dedicated parallel-lines coordinate-geometry node.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Covers geometrical terms, triangles, quadrilaterals, polygons, nets, solids and circle vocabulary; nets and circle vocabulary have no specific nodes.
Measure/draw lines and angles, construct triangles; nets are not represented by a specific node.
Scale drawings and three-figure bearings; bearings and scale-drawing nodes are absent from the catalog.
Calculate lengths of similar shapes using length scale factors.
Line symmetry and order of rotational symmetry in 2D.
Angle properties at a point, on a straight line, vertically opposite, parallel lines, triangles/quadrilaterals and regular polygons; polygon angle properties only partly covered.
Only angle in a semicircle and tangent-radius theorem are required at Core.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Metric units of length, area, volume, capacity and conversions between them.
Perimeter and area of rectangle, triangle, parallelogram and trapezium.
Circumference, area of a circle, arc length and sector area as fractions of circle measures.
Includes sphere and cone as well as cuboid, prism, cylinder, pyramid; sphere/cone nodes are absent from the catalog.
Compound shapes/parts and compound solids/parts; mapped to parent mensuration nodes because no compound-shape nodes exist.
Know and use Pythagoras' theorem.
Sine, cosine and tangent ratios for acute angles and 2D problem solving including Pythagoras.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Reflections, rotations, enlargements (positive and fractional scale factors) and translations by column vectors; combinations excluded.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Placeholder for Extended content only; no Core mapping.
Probability notation explicitly excluded in official notes; mapped to complement rather than formal notation node.
Relative frequency and expected frequency are not represented by specific canonical nodes; mapped to the broader single-event probability node.
Combined events with replacement only; Venn diagrams limited to two sets.
Classify and tabulate statistical data including tally and two-way tables.
Restrictions on drawing conclusions are not specifically represented; mapped to interpretation and comparison nodes.
Individual data only; grouped data excluded at Core tier.
Includes composite and dual bar charts; stem-and-leaf diagrams require ordered data with a key.
Line of best fit drawn by inspection across full data set with roughly even point distribution.
Rational/irrational numbers and reciprocals are listed in the syllabus but lack specific canonical nodes.
Venn diagrams limited to two or three sets; standard set notation listed.
Includes recall of squares/cubes and roots up to specified values.
Includes recurring decimal notation and conversion between recurring decimals and fractions.
Use of equality and inequality symbols.
Four operations with integers, fractions and decimals including correct order of operations and brackets.
Positive, zero, negative and fractional indices including rules of indices.
Standard form A x 10^n with 1 <= A < 10 and n positive or negative integer.
Rounding to decimal places and significant figures; estimation in context.
Upper and lower bounds for rounded data and for results of calculations.
Ratio in simplest form, dividing a quantity in a ratio, proportional reasoning in context.
Flow rates, fuel consumption, exchange rates, hourly rates and population density lack specific nodes.
Simple and compound interest and reverse percentages are not represented by specific nodes.
Calculator efficiency, appropriate entry and interpretation of display; no dedicated calculator-use node.
Time calculations across units, 12/24-hour clocks, timetables and time zones.
Money calculations and currency conversion.
Exponential growth and decay including depreciation and population change; knowledge of e not required.
Simplifying surds and rationalising denominators.
Letters as generalised numbers and substitution into expressions and formulas.
Includes expanding products of more than two brackets, factorising including cubics, and completing the square.
No dedicated algebraic-fractions node; partial fractions node is a broader A-level analogue.
Indices including solving simple exponential equations; logarithms not required.
Includes fractional equations with linear denominators and changing subject where subject appears twice or with powers/roots.
Linear inequalities in one variable and in two variables graphically; linear programming not included.
Linear, quadratic, cubic and exponential sequences and simple combinations.
Direct and inverse proportion including linear, square, square root, cube and cube root proportion.
Practical graphs, rate of change and kinematics including distance as area under speed-time graph.
Graphs of ax^n and ab^x+c; solving associated equations graphically including intersections of line and curve.
Sketching and interpreting linear, quadratic, cubic, reciprocal and exponential graphs; asymptotes and turning points.
Differentiation of ax^n and simple sums; stationary points and discriminating maxima/minima; points of inflection excluded.
Function notation, domain and range, inverse functions and composite functions; domains/ranges of composite functions excluded.
Covers use and interpretation of Cartesian coordinates in two dimensions.
Drawing straight-line graphs from linear equations, including general form.
Includes finding the gradient of a line and calculating it from two points.
Calculates length and midpoint of a line segment.
Interpret and obtain equations in different forms and identify gradient/y-intercept.
Parallel line equations rely on equal gradients; no dedicated parallel-line coordinate geometry node exists.
Perpendicular gradient relationship and perpendicular bisector are not separately represented; midpoint node used for bisector aspect.
Broad vocabulary point; terms such as point, vertex, line, plane, bearing, nets, solids and circle vocabulary lack specific nodes.
Covers measuring/drawing lines and angles and triangle construction. Nets are not represented in the node catalog; perpendicular/angle bisectors are explicitly excluded.
Scale drawings and three-figure bearings; bearings are not represented by a dedicated node.
Length and area scale factors are represented; volume scale factor and similarity proofs are not separately represented.
Covers line/rotational symmetry in 2D and planes/axes of symmetry in solids.
Angle properties at a point, on a line, vertically opposite and parallel lines are covered. Triangle/quadrilateral angle sums and polygon angle properties are not represented by dedicated nodes.
All six listed circle theorems are represented by dedicated nodes.
Equal chords and tangent length properties are covered; perpendicular bisector of a chord is not separately represented.
Metric units and conversions for length, area, volume and capacity.
Perimeter and area calculations for rectangle, triangle, parallelogram and trapezium.
Circumference, area, arc length and sector area of circles.
Surface area and volume of cuboids, prisms, cylinders and pyramids are represented; sphere and cone are not represented by dedicated nodes.
Compound shapes and parts of shapes/solids; mapped to parent area and volume nodes because compound-shape problem solving is not separately represented.
Know and use Pythagoras theorem.
Right-angled triangle ratios and 2D problem solving are covered. Angles of elevation/depression and perpendicular distance are not represented by dedicated nodes.
Exact trigonometric values for standard angles.
Recognise and sketch sin/cos/tan graphs and solve simple trigonometric equations over 0-360 degrees.
Sine rule, cosine rule and area formula for any triangle.
3D Pythagoras/trigonometry and angle between a line and a plane are not represented by dedicated nodes.
Reflections, rotations, enlargements (including fractional/negative scale factors), translations and combinations.
Vector notation, addition, subtraction and scalar multiplication in two dimensions.
Calculate magnitude of a column vector.
Position vectors, directed line segments and vector reasoning including parallel vectors, collinearity, ratio and similarity.
Probability scale, notation, single events and complement.
Relative frequency as an estimate of probability and expected frequencies are not represented by dedicated nodes.
Combined events using sample space, Venn and tree diagrams.
Conditional probability calculated from diagrams/tables without formal P(A|B) notation.
Classify and tabulate statistical data.
Interpret tables/diagrams and compare data sets. Restrictions on drawing conclusions are not represented by a dedicated node.
Averages, spread, grouped-data mean estimate and modal class.
Bar, pie, pictogram and stem-and-leaf diagrams; simple frequency distributions are not separately represented.
Scatter diagrams, correlation types and line of best fit.
Cumulative frequency tables/diagrams and estimation of median, percentiles, quartiles and interquartile range.
Histograms and frequency density calculations for grouped data.
